verses.latin_vulgate: Et rex David senuerat, habebatque ætatis plurimos dies : cumque operiretur vestibus, non calefiebat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמֶּ֤לֶךְ דָּוִד֙ זָקֵ֔ן בָּ֖א בַּיָּמִ֑ים וַיְכַסֻּ֙הוּ֙ בַּבְּגָדִ֔ים וְלֹ֥א יִחַ֖ם לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now king David was old, and advanced in years: and when he was covered with clothes he was not warm.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin kuningas David oli vanhentunut ja ijälliseksi joutunut, ei hän voinut itsiänsä lämmittää, ehkä hän vaatteilla peitettiin.
verses.king_james: Now king David was old and stricken in years; and they covered him with clothes, but he gat no heat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo ei servi sui : Quæramus domino nostro regi adolescentulam virginem, et stet coram rege, et foveat eum, dormiatque in sinu suo, et calefaciat dominum nostrum regem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמְרוּ ל֣וֹ עֲבָדָ֗יו יְבַקְשׁ֞וּ לַאדֹנִ֤י הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ נַעֲרָ֣ה בְתוּלָ֔ה וְעָֽמְדָה֙ לִפְנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וּתְהִי־ל֖וֹ סֹכֶ֑נֶת וְשָׁכְבָ֣ה בְחֵיקֶ֔ךָ וְחַ֖ם לַאדֹנִ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: His servants therefore, said to him: Let us seek for our Lord the king, a young virgin, and let her stand before the king, and cherish him, and sleep in his bosom and warm our lord the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoivat hänen palveliansa hänelle: anna heidän etsiä herralleni kuninkaalle piika, joka neitsy on, joka seisois kuninkaan edessä, ja korjaisi häntä ja makais hänen sylissänsä, ja lämmittäis herraani, kuningasta.
verses.king_james: Wherefore his servants said unto him, Let there be sought for my lord the king a young virgin: and let her stand before the king, and let her cherish him, and let her lie in thy bosom, that my lord the king may get heat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæsierunt igitur adolescentulam speciosam in omnibus finibus Israël, et invenerunt Abisag Sunamitidem, et adduxerunt eam ad regem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְבַקְשׁוּ֙ נַעֲרָ֣ה יָפָ֔ה בְּכֹ֖ל גְּב֣וּל יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַֽיִּמְצְא֗וּ אֶת־אֲבִישַׁג֙ הַשּׁ֣וּנַמִּ֔ית וַיָּבִ֥אוּ אֹתָ֖הּ לַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So they sought a beautiful young woman, in all the coasts of Israel and they found Abisag, a Sunamitess, and brought her to the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he etsivät ihanan piian kaikista Israelin rajoista; ja he löysivät Abisagin Sunemista, ja veivät hänet kuninkaan tykö.
verses.king_james: So they sought for a fair damsel throughout all the coasts of Israel, and found Abishag a Shunammite, and brought her to the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem puella pulchra nimis, dormiebatque cum rege, et ministrabat ei : rex vero non cognovit eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַֽנַּעֲרָ֖ה יָפָ֣ה עַד־מְאֹ֑ד וַתְּהִ֨י לַמֶּ֤לֶךְ סֹכֶ֙נֶת֙ וַתְּשָׁ֣רְתֵ֔הוּ וְהַמֶּ֖לֶךְ לֹ֥א יְדָעָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the damsel was exceedingly beautiful, and she slept with the king, and served him, but the king did not know her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja se oli sangen ihanainen piika, ja hän korjasi kuningasta ja palveli häntä, vaan ei kuningas tuntenut häntä.
verses.king_james: And the damsel was very fair, and cherished the king, and ministered to him: but the king knew her not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adonias autem filius Haggith elevabatur, dicens : Ego regnabo. Fecitque sibi currus et equites, et quinquaginta viros qui currerent ante eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲדֹנִיָּ֧ה בֶן־חַגִּ֛ית מִתְנַשֵּׂ֥א לֵאמֹ֖ר אֲנִ֣י אֶמְלֹ֑ךְ וַיַּ֣עַשׂ ל֗וֹ רֶ֚כֶב וּפָ֣רָשִׁ֔ים וַחֲמִשִּׁ֥ים אִ֖ישׁ רָצִ֥ים לְפָנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Adonias, the son of Haggith, exalted himself, saying: I will be king. And he made himself chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Adonia Haggitin poika korotti itsensä ja sanoi: minä tahdon olla kuningas; ja rakensi itsellensä vaunut ja hevosmiehiä, ja viisikymmentä miestä juoksemaan edellänsä.
verses.king_james: Then Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, I will be king: and he prepared him chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec corripuit eum pater suus aliquando, dicens : Quare hoc fecisti ? Erat autem et ipse pulcher valde, secundus natu post Absalom.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹֽא־עֲצָב֨וֹ אָבִ֤יו מִיָּמָיו֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר מַדּ֖וּעַ כָּ֣כָה עָשִׂ֑יתָ וְגַם־ה֤וּא טֽוֹב־תֹּ֙אַר֙ מְאֹ֔ד וְאֹת֥וֹ יָלְדָ֖ה אַחֲרֵ֥י אַבְשָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Neither did his father rebuke him at any time, saying: Why hast thou done this? And he also was very beautiful, the next in birth after Absalom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänen isänsä ei tahtonut tehdä häntä surulliseksi eläissänsä, että hän olis sanonut: kuinkas niin teet? Ja hän oli myös sangen ihanainen; ja oli hänen synnyttänyt Absalomin jälkeen.
verses.king_james: And his father had not displeased him at any time in saying, Why hast thou done so? and he also was a very goodly man; and his mother bare him after Absalom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sermo ei cum Joab filio Sarviæ, et cum Abiathar sacerdote, qui adjuvabant partes Adoniæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּהְי֣וּ דְבָרָ֔יו עִ֚ם יוֹאָ֣ב בֶּן־צְרוּיָ֔ה וְעִ֖ם אֶבְיָתָ֣ר הַכֹּהֵ֑ן וַֽיַּעְזְר֔וּ אַחֲרֵ֖י אֲדֹנִיָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he conferred with Joab, the son of Sarvia, and with Abiathar, the priest, who furthered Adonias's side.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän puhui Joabin ZeruJan pojan kanssa ja papin Abjatarin kanssa; ja he auttivat Adoniaa.
verses.king_james: And he conferred with Joab the son of Zeruiah, and with Abiathar the priest: and they following Adonijah helped him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sadoc vero sacerdos, et Banaias filius Jojadæ, et Nathan propheta, et Semei et Rei, et robur exercitus David, non erat cum Adonia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְצָד֣וֹק הַ֠כֹּהֵ֠ן וּבְנָיָ֨הוּ בֶן־יְהוֹיָדָ֜ע וְנָתָ֤ן הַנָּבִיא֙ וְשִׁמְעִ֣י וְרֵעִ֔י וְהַגִּבּוֹרִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְדָוִ֑ד לֹ֥א הָי֖וּ עִם־אֲדֹנִיָּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Sadoc, the priest, and Banaias, the son of Joiada, and Nathan, the prophet, and Semei, and Rei, and the strength of David's army, was not with Adonias.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Zadok pappi, ja BenaJa Jojadan poika, ja Natan propheta, ja Simei, ja Rei, ja Davidin sankarit ei olleet Adonian kanssa.
verses.king_james: But Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and Nathan the prophet, and Shimei, and Rei, and the mighty men which belonged to David, were not with Adonijah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Immolatis ergo Adonias arietibus et vitulis, et universis pinguibus, juxta lapidem Zoheleth, qui erat vicinus fonti Rogel, vocavit universos fratres suos filios regis, et omnes viros Juda servos regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּזְבַּ֣ח אֲדֹנִיָּ֗הוּ צֹ֤אן וּבָקָר֙ וּמְרִ֔יא עִ֚ם אֶ֣בֶן הַזֹּחֶ֔לֶת אֲשֶׁר־אֵ֖צֶל עֵ֣ין רֹגֵ֑ל וַיִּקְרָ֗א אֶת־כׇּל־אֶחָיו֙ בְּנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וּלְכׇל־אַנְשֵׁ֥י יְהוּדָ֖ה עַבְדֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Adonias having slain rams and calves, and all fat cattle, by the stone of Zoheleth, which was near the fountain Rogel, invited all his brethren, the king's sons, and all the men of Juda, the king's servants:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Adonia uhrasi lampaita ja härkiä ja myös syötettyä karjaa Soheletin kiven tykönä, joka on läsnä Rogelin kaivoa, kutsui hän kaikki veljensä, kuninkaan pojat, ja kaikki Juudan miehet kuninkaan palveliat.
verses.king_james: And Adonijah slew sheep and oxen and fat cattle by the stone of Zoheleth, which is by En–rogel, and called all his brethren the king’s sons, and all the men of Judah the king’s servants:

verses.latin_vulgate: Nathan autem prophetam, et Banaiam, et robustos quosque, et Salomonem fratrem suum non vocavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽאֶת־נָתָן֩ הַנָּבִ֨יא וּבְנָיָ֜הוּ וְֽאֶת־הַגִּבּוֹרִ֛ים וְאֶת־שְׁלֹמֹ֥ה אָחִ֖יו לֹ֥א קָרָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Nathan, the prophet, and Banaias, and all the valiant men, and Solomon, his brother, he invited not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Natan prophetaa, ja Benajaa ja sankareita, ja veljeänsä Salomoa hän ei kutsunut.
verses.king_james: But Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, and the mighty men, and Solomon his brother, he called not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit itaque Nathan ad Bethsabee matrem Salomonis : Num audisti quod regnaverit Adonias filius Haggith, et dominus noster David hoc ignorat ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר נָתָ֗ן אֶל־בַּת־שֶׁ֤בַע אֵם־שְׁלֹמֹה֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הֲל֣וֹא שָׁמַ֔עַתְּ כִּ֥י מָלַ֖ךְ אֲדֹנִיָּ֣הוּ בֶן־חַגִּ֑ית וַאֲדֹנֵ֥ינוּ דָוִ֖ד לֹ֥א יָדָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Nathan said to Bethsabee, the mother of Solomon: Hast thou not heard that Adonias, the son of Haggith, reigneth, and our lord David knoweth it not?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin puhui Natan BatSeballe Salomon äidille, sanoen: etkös ole kuullut Adoniaa Haggitin poikaa tulleeksi kuninkaaksi? ja herramme David ei siitä mitään tiedä;
verses.king_james: Wherefore Nathan spake unto Bath–sheba the mother of Solomon, saying, Hast thou not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith doth reign, and David our lord knoweth it not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo veni, accipe consilium a me, et salva animam tuam, filiique tui Salomonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֕ה לְכִ֛י אִיעָצֵ֥ךְ נָ֖א עֵצָ֑ה וּמַלְּטִי֙ אֶת־נַפְשֵׁ֔ךְ וְאֶת־נֶ֥פֶשׁ בְּנֵ֖ךְ שְׁלֹמֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now then, come, take my counsel, and save thy life, and the life of thy son Solomon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tule nyt, minä annan sinulle neuvoa, ettäs vapahtaisit sielus ja poikas Salomon sielun.
verses.king_james: Now therefore come, let me, I pray thee, give thee counsel, that thou mayest save thine own life, and the life of thy son Solomon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade, et ingredere ad regem David, et dic ei : Nonne tu, domine mi rex, jurasti mihi ancillæ tuæ, dicens : Salomon filius tuus regnabit post me, et ipse sedebit in solio meo ? quare ergo regnat Adonias ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְכִ֞י וּבֹ֣אִי ׀ אֶל־הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ דָּוִ֗ד וְאָמַ֤רְתְּ אֵלָיו֙ הֲלֹֽא־אַתָּ֞ה אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֗לֶךְ נִשְׁבַּ֤עְתָּ לַאֲמָֽתְךָ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר כִּֽי־שְׁלֹמֹ֤ה בְנֵךְ֙ יִמְלֹ֣ךְ אַחֲרַ֔י וְה֖וּא יֵשֵׁ֣ב עַל־כִּסְאִ֑י וּמַדּ֖וּעַ מָלַ֥ךְ אֲדֹנִיָּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Go, and get thee in to king David, and say to him: Didst not thou, my lord, O king, swear to me, thy handmaid, saying: Solomon, thy son, shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne? why then doth Adonias reign?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mene ja käy kuningas Davidin tykö, ja sano hänelle: herrani kuningas, etkös ole vannonut ja sanonut piialles: sinun poikas Salomo pitää oleman kuningas minun jälkeeni ja istuman minun istuimellani? Miksi siis Adonia on kuninkaaksi tullut?
verses.king_james: Go and get thee in unto king David, and say unto him, Didst not thou, my lord, O king, swear unto thine handmaid, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne? why then doth Adonijah reign?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et adhuc ibi te loquente cum rege, ego veniam post te, et complebo sermones tuos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֗ה עוֹדָ֛ךְ מְדַבֶּ֥רֶת שָׁ֖ם עִם־הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וַֽאֲנִי֙ אָב֣וֹא אַחֲרַ֔יִךְ וּמִלֵּאתִ֖י אֶת־דְּבָרָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And while thou art yet speaking there with the king, I will come in after thee, and will fill up thy words.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, sinun vielä siellä puhuissas kuninkaan kanssa, tulen minä myös perässäs ja täytän puhees.
verses.king_james: Behold, while thou yet talkest there with the king, I also will come in after thee, and confirm thy words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ingressa est itaque Bethsabee ad regem in cubiculum : rex autem senuerat nimis, et Abisag Sunamitis ministrabat ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּבֹ֨א בַת־שֶׁ֤בַע אֶל־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ הַחַ֔דְרָה וְהַמֶּ֖לֶךְ זָקֵ֣ן מְאֹ֑ד וַאֲבִישַׁג֙ הַשּׁ֣וּנַמִּ֔ית מְשָׁרַ֖ת אֶת־הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Bethsabee went in to the king into the chamber. Now the king was very old, and Abisag, the Sunamitess, ministered to him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja BatSeba meni kamariin kuninkaan tykö, ja kuningas oli juuri vanha; ja Abisag Sunemista palveli Kuningasta.
verses.king_james: And Bath–sheba went in unto the king into the chamber: and the king was very old; and Abishag the Shunammite ministered unto the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inclinavit se Bethsabee, et adoravit regem. Ad quam rex : Quid tibi, inquit, vis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּקֹּ֣ד בַּת־שֶׁ֔בַע וַתִּשְׁתַּ֖חוּ לַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וַיֹּ֥אמֶר הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ מַה־לָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Bethsabee bowed herself, and worshipped the king. And the king said to her: What is thy will?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja BatSeba nöyryytti itsensä ja kumarsi kuningasta. Kuningas sanoi: mitäs tahdot?
verses.king_james: And Bath–sheba bowed, and did obeisance unto the king. And the king said, What wouldest thou?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ respondens, ait : Domine mi, tu jurasti per Dominum Deum tuum ancillæ tuæ : Salomon filius tuus regnabit post me, et ipse sedebit in solio meo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ אֲדֹנִי֙ אַתָּ֨ה נִשְׁבַּ֜עְתָּ בַּיהֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהֶ֙יךָ֙ לַאֲמָתֶ֔ךָ כִּֽי־שְׁלֹמֹ֥ה בְנֵ֖ךְ יִמְלֹ֣ךְ אַחֲרָ֑י וְה֖וּא יֵשֵׁ֥ב עַל־כִּסְאִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: She answered, and said: My lord, thou didst swear to thy handmaid, by the Lord thy God, saying: Solomon, thy son, shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi hänelle: herrani, sinä vannoit piialles Herran sinun Jumalas kautta: sinun poikas Salomo pitää oleman kuningas minun jälkeeni ja istuman minun istuimellani?
verses.king_james: And she said unto him, My lord, thou swarest by the Lord thy God unto thine handmaid, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce nunc Adonias regnat, te, domine mi rex, ignorante.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֕ה הִנֵּ֥ה אֲדֹנִיָּ֖ה מָלָ֑ךְ וְעַתָּ֛ה אֲדֹנִ֥י הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ לֹ֥א יָדָֽעְתָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And behold, now Adonias reigneth, and thou, my lord the king, knowest nothing of it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta nyt, katso, Adonia on tullut kuninkaaksi, ja herrani kuningas, et sinä siitä mitään tiedä.
verses.king_james: And now, behold, Adonijah reigneth; and now, my lord the king, thou knowest it not:

verses.latin_vulgate: Mactavit boves, et pinguia quæque, et arietes plurimos, et vocavit omnes filios regis, Abiathar quoque sacerdotem, et Joab principem militiæ : Salomonem autem servum tuum non vocavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֠יִּזְבַּ֠ח שׁ֥וֹר וּֽמְרִיא־וְצֹאן֮ לָרֹב֒ {פ}וַיִּקְרָא֙ לְכׇל־בְּנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וּלְאֶבְיָתָר֙ הַכֹּהֵ֔ן וּלְיֹאָ֖ב שַׂ֣ר הַצָּבָ֑א וְלִשְׁלֹמֹ֥ה עַבְדְּךָ֖ לֹ֥א קָרָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath killed oxen, and all fat cattle, and many rams, and invited all the king's sons, and Abiathar, the priest, and Joab, the general of the army: but Solomon, thy servant, he invited not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on uhrannut härkiä ja syötettyä karjaa, ja monta lammasta, ja on kutsunut kaikki kuninkaan pojat, ja papin AbJatarin, ja Joabin sodanpäämiehen; mutta palveliaas Salomoa ei hän kutsunut.
verses.king_james: And he hath slain oxen and fat cattle and sheep in abundance, and hath called all the sons of the king, and Abiathar the priest, and Joab the captain of the host: but Solomon thy servant hath he not called.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen, domine mi rex, in te oculi respiciunt totius Israël, ut indices eis quis sedere debeat in solio tuo, domine mi rex, post te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַתָּה֙ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ עֵינֵ֥י כׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל עָלֶ֑יךָ לְהַגִּ֣יד לָהֶ֔ם מִ֗י יֵשֵׁ֛ב עַל־כִּסֵּ֥א אֲדֹנִֽי־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ אַחֲרָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And now, my lord, O king, the eyes of all Israel are upon thee, that thou shouldst tell them, who shall sit on thy throne, my lord the king, after thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta sinä, Herrani kuningas, koko Israelin silmät katsovat sinun päälles, ettäs ilmoittaisit heidän edessänsä, kuka on istuva herrani kuninkaan istuimella hänen jälkeensä.
verses.king_james: And thou, my lord, O king, the eyes of all Israel are upon thee, that thou shouldest tell them who shall sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eritque, cum dormierit dominus meus rex cum patribus suis, erimus ego et filius meus Salomon peccatores.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֕ה כִּשְׁכַ֥ב אֲדֹנִֽי־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ עִם־אֲבֹתָ֑יו וְהָיִ֗יתִי אֲנִ֛י וּבְנִ֥י שְׁלֹמֹ֖ה חַטָּאִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Otherwise it shall come to pass, when my lord the king sleepeth with his fathers, that I, and my son, Solomon, shall be accounted offenders.
verses.finnish_biblia: Muutoin tapahtuu, kuin herrani kuningas isäinsä kanssa nukkunut on, niin täytyy minun ja poikani Salomon vialliset olla.
verses.king_james: Otherwise it shall come to pass, when my lord the king shall sleep with his fathers, that I and my son Solomon shall be counted offenders.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc illa loquente cum rege, Nathan propheta venit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִנֵּ֛ה עוֹדֶ֥נָּה מְדַבֶּ֖רֶת עִם־הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וְנָתָ֥ן הַנָּבִ֖יא בָּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: As she was yet speaking with the king, Nathan, the prophet, came.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katso, vielä hänen puhuissansa kuninkaan kanssa, tuli Natan propheta.
verses.king_james: And, lo, while she yet talked with the king, Nathan the prophet also came in.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nuntiaverunt regi, dicentes : Adest Nathan propheta. Cumque introisset in conspectu regis, et adorasset eum pronus in terram,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּגִּ֤ידוּ לַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הִנֵּ֖ה נָתָ֣ן הַנָּבִ֑יא וַיָּבֹא֙ לִפְנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַיִּשְׁתַּ֧חוּ לַמֶּ֛לֶךְ עַל־אַפָּ֖יו אָֽרְצָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they told the king, saying: Nathan, the prophet, is here. And when he was come in before the king, and had worshipped, bowing down to the ground,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he ilmoittivat kuninkaalle sanoen: katso, Natan propheta on täällä. Ja kuin hän tuli sisälle kuninkaan eteen, lankesi hän maahan kasvoillensa kuninkaan eteen.
verses.king_james: And they told the king, saying, Behold Nathan the prophet. And when he was come in before the king, he bowed himself before the king with his face to the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit Nathan : Domine mi rex, tu dixisti : Adonias regnet post me, et ipse sedeat super thronum meum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֘אמֶר֮ נָתָן֒ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ אַתָּ֣ה אָמַ֔רְתָּ אֲדֹנִיָּ֖הוּ יִמְלֹ֣ךְ אַחֲרָ֑י וְה֖וּא יֵשֵׁ֥ב עַל־כִּסְאִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Nathan said: My lord, O king, hast thou said: Let Adonias reign after me, and let him sit upon my throne?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Natan sanoi: herrani kuningas, sanoitkos: Adonia pitää oleman kuningas minun jälkeeni ja istuman minun istuimellani?
verses.king_james: And Nathan said, My lord, O king, hast thou said, Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia descendit hodie, et immolavit boves, et pinguia, et arietes plurimos, et vocavit universos filios regis et principes exercitus, Abiathar quoque sacerdotem, illisque vescentibus et bibentibus coram eo, et dicentibus : Vivat rex Adonias :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י ׀ יָרַ֣ד הַיּ֗וֹם וַ֠יִּזְבַּ֠ח שׁ֥וֹר וּֽמְרִיא־וְצֹאן֮ לָרֹב֒ וַיִּקְרָא֩ לְכׇל־בְּנֵ֨י הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ וּלְשָׂרֵ֤י הַצָּבָא֙ וּלְאֶבְיָתָ֣ר הַכֹּהֵ֔ן וְהִנָּ֛ם אֹכְלִ֥ים וְשֹׁתִ֖ים לְפָנָ֑יו וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ יְחִ֖י הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ אֲדֹנִיָּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because he is gone down to day, and hath killed oxen, and fatlings, and many rams, and invited all the king's sons, and the captains of the army, and Abiathar the priest: and they are eating and drinking before him, and saying: God save king Adonias:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä hän on tänäpänä mennyt ja uhrannut härkiä, ja syötettyjä karjaa, ja monta lammasta, ja on kutsunut kaikki kuninkaan pojat ja sodanpäämiehet, niin myös papin AbJatarin, ja katso, he syövät ja juovat hänen edessänsä, ja sanovat: onneksi kuninkaalle Adonialle!
verses.king_james: For he is gone down this day, and hath slain oxen and fat cattle and sheep in abundance, and hath called all the king’s sons, and the captains of the host, and Abiathar the priest; and, behold, they eat and drink before him, and say, God save king Adonijah.

verses.latin_vulgate: me servum tuum, et Sadoc sacerdotem, et Banaiam filium Jojadæ, et Salomonem famulum tuum non vocavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלִ֣י אֲנִֽי־עַ֠בְדֶּ֠ךָ וּלְצָדֹ֨ק הַכֹּהֵ֜ן וְלִבְנָיָ֧הוּ בֶן־יְהוֹיָדָ֛ע וְלִשְׁלֹמֹ֥ה עַבְדְּךָ֖ לֹ֥א קָרָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: But me, thy servant, and Sadoc, the priest, and Banaias, the son of Joiada, and Solomon, thy servant, he hath not invited.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta minua sinun palveliaas, ja pappia Zadokia, ja BenaJaa Jojadan poikaa, ja palveliaas Salomoa ei ole hän kutsunut.
verses.king_james: But me, even me thy servant, and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and thy servant Solomon, hath he not called.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid a domino meo rege exivit hoc verbum, et mihi non indicasti servo tuo quis sessurus esset super thronum domini mei regis post eum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֗ם מֵאֵת֙ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ נִֽהְיָ֖ה הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֑ה וְלֹ֤א הוֹדַ֙עְתָּ֙ אֶֽת־[עַבְדְּךָ֔] (עבדיך) מִ֗י יֵשֵׁ֛ב עַל־כִּסֵּ֥א אֲדֹנִֽי־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ אַחֲרָֽיו׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Is this word come out from my lord the king, and hast thou not told me, thy servant, who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?
verses.finnish_biblia: Onko se niin herraltani kuninkaalta käsketty, ja et sinä ole sitä asiata antanut tietää palvelialles, kuka herrani kuninkaan istuimella hänen jälkeensä on istuva?
verses.king_james: Is this thing done by my lord the king, and thou hast not shewed it unto thy servant, who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondit rex David, dicens : Vocate ad me Bethsabee. Quæ cum fuisset ingressa coram rege, et stetisset ante eum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֨עַן הַמֶּ֤לֶךְ דָּוִד֙ וַיֹּ֔אמֶר קִרְאוּ־לִ֖י לְבַת־שָׁ֑בַע וַתָּבֹא֙ לִפְנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַֽתַּעֲמֹ֖ד לִפְנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And king David answered, and said: Call to me Bethsabee. And when she was come in to the king, and stood before him,
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuningas David vastasi ja sanoi: kutsukaat minulle BatSeba. Ja hän tuli kuninkaan eteen ja seisoi kuninkaan edessä.
verses.king_james: Then king David answered and said, Call me Bath–sheba. And she came into the king’s presence, and stood before the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: juravit rex, et ait : Vivit Dominus, qui eruit animam meam de omni angustia,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׁבַ֥ע הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר חַי־יְהֹוָ֕ה אֲשֶׁר־פָּדָ֥ה אֶת־נַפְשִׁ֖י מִכׇּל־צָרָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The king swore, and said: As the Lord liveth, who hath delivered my soul out of all distress,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas vannoi ja sanoi: niin totta kuin Herra elää, joka minun sieluni on päästänyt kaikkinaisesta ahdistuksesta;
verses.king_james: And the king sware, and said, As the Lord liveth, that hath redeemed my soul out of all distress,

verses.latin_vulgate: quia sicut juravi tibi per Dominum Deum Israël, dicens : Salomon filius tuus regnabit post me, et ipse sedebit super solium meum pro me : sic faciam hodie.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֡י כַּאֲשֶׁר֩ נִשְׁבַּ֨עְתִּי לָ֜ךְ בַּיהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהֵ֤י יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר כִּֽי־שְׁלֹמֹ֤ה בְנֵךְ֙ יִמְלֹ֣ךְ אַחֲרַ֔י וְה֛וּא יֵשֵׁ֥ב עַל־כִּסְאִ֖י תַּחְתָּ֑י כִּ֛י כֵּ֥ן אֶעֱשֶׂ֖ה הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Even as I swore to thee, by the Lord, the God of Israel, saying: Solomon thy son, shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne in my stead, so will I do this day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin minä sinulle Herran Israelin Jumalan edessä vannonut olen ja sanonut: Salomo sinun poikas pitää oleman kuningas minun jälkeeni, ja hän pitää istuman minun istuimellani minun edestäni; niin minä teen tänäpänä.
verses.king_james: Even as I sware unto thee by the Lord God of Israel, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne in my stead; even so will I certainly do this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Summissoque Bethsabee in terram vultu, adoravit regem, dicens : Vivat dominus meus David in æternum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּקֹּ֨ד בַּת־שֶׁ֤בַע אַפַּ֙יִם֙ אֶ֔רֶץ וַתִּשְׁתַּ֖חוּ לַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וַתֹּ֕אמֶר יְחִ֗י אֲדֹנִ֛י הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ דָּוִ֖ד לְעֹלָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Bethsabee, bowing with her face to the earth, worshipped the king, saying: May my lord David live for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin BatSeba lankesi kasvoillensa maahan ja kumarsi kuningasta, sanoen: onneksi herralleni kuningas Davidille ijankaikkisesti!
verses.king_james: Then Bath–sheba bowed with her face to the earth, and did reverence to the king, and said, Let my lord king David live for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit quoque rex David : Vocate mihi Sadoc sacerdotem, et Nathan prophetam, et Banaiam filium Jojadæ. Qui cum ingressi fuissent coram rege,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ׀ הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ דָּוִ֗ד קִרְאוּ־לִ֞י לְצָד֤וֹק הַכֹּהֵן֙ וּלְנָתָ֣ן הַנָּבִ֔יא וְלִבְנָיָ֖הוּ בֶּן־יְהוֹיָדָ֑ע וַיָּבֹ֖אוּ לִפְנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: King David also said: Call me Sadoc, the priest, and Nathan, the prophet, and Banaias, the son of Joiada. And when they were come in before the king,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas David sanoi: kutsukaat Zadok pappi, Natan propheta ja BenaJa Jojadan poika. Ja he tulivat kuninkaan eteen.
verses.king_james: And king David said, Call me Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada. And they came before the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit ad eos : Tollite vobiscum servos domini vestri, et imponite Salomonem filium meum super mulam meam, et ducite eum in Gihon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ לָהֶ֗ם קְח֤וּ עִמָּכֶם֙ אֶת־עַבְדֵ֣י אֲדֹנֵיכֶ֔ם וְהִרְכַּבְתֶּם֙ אֶת־שְׁלֹמֹ֣ה בְנִ֔י עַל־הַפִּרְדָּ֖ה אֲשֶׁר־לִ֑י וְהוֹרַדְתֶּ֥ם אֹת֖וֹ אֶל־גִּחֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said to them: Take with you the servants of your lord, and set my son Solomon upon my mule: and bring him to Gihon:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas sanoi heille: ottakaat kanssanne teidän herranne palveliat, ja asettakaat poikani Salomo minun muulini päälle, ja viekäät häntä Gihoniin,
verses.king_james: The king also said unto them, Take with you the servants of your lord, and cause Solomon my son to ride upon mine own mule, and bring him down to Gihon:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ungat eum ibi Sadoc sacerdos et Nathan propheta in regem super Israël : et canetis buccina, atque dicetis : Vivat rex Salomon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמָשַׁ֣ח אֹת֣וֹ שָׁ֠ם צָד֨וֹק הַכֹּהֵ֜ן וְנָתָ֧ן הַנָּבִ֛יא לְמֶ֖לֶךְ עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וּתְקַעְתֶּם֙ בַּשּׁוֹפָ֔ר וַאֲמַרְתֶּ֕ם יְחִ֖י הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And let Sadoc, the priest, and Nathan, the prophet, anoint him there king over Israel: and you shall sound the trumpet, and shall say: God save king Solomon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Zadok pappi ja Natan propheta voidelkaan hänen Israelin kuninkaaksi. Ja soittakaat basunalla ja sanokaat: onneksi kuninkaalle Salomolle!
verses.king_james: And let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there king over Israel: and blow ye with the trumpet, and say, God save king Solomon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendetis post eum, et veniet, et sedebit super solium meum, et ipse regnabit pro me : illique præcipiam ut sit dux super Israël et super Judam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַעֲלִיתֶ֣ם אַחֲרָ֗יו וּבָא֙ וְיָשַׁ֣ב עַל־כִּסְאִ֔י וְה֥וּא יִמְלֹ֖ךְ תַּחְתָּ֑י וְאֹת֤וֹ צִוִּ֙יתִי֙ לִֽהְי֣וֹת נָגִ֔יד עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל וְעַל־יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you shall come up after him, and he shall come, and shall sit upon my throne, and he shall reign in my stead: and I will appoint him to be ruler over Israel, and over Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja menkäät ylös hänen perässänsä, että hän tulis ja istuis minun istuimellani, ja olis kuningas minun edestäni: ja minä käsken hänen olla Israelin ja Juudan päämiehenä.
verses.king_james: Then ye shall come up after him, that he may come and sit upon my throne; for he shall be king in my stead: and I have appointed him to be ruler over Israel and over Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondit Banaias filius Jojadæ regi, dicens : Amen : sic loquatur Dominus Deus domini mei regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֨עַן בְּנָיָ֧הוּ בֶן־יְהוֹיָדָ֛ע אֶת־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ׀ אָמֵ֑ן כֵּ֚ן יֹאמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה אֱלֹהֵ֖י אֲדֹנִ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Banaias, the son of Joiada, answered the king, saying: Amen: so say the Lord, the God of my lord the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vastasi BenaJa Jojadan poika kuninkaalle ja sanoi: Amen! Sanokaan myös niin Herra, herrani kuninkaan Jumala!
verses.king_james: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king, and said, Amen: the Lord God of my lord the king say so too.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo fuit Dominus cum domino meo rege, sic sit cum Salomone, et sublimius faciat solium ejus a solio domini mei regis David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּאֲשֶׁ֨ר הָיָ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ עִם־אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ כֵּ֖ן (יהי) [יִהְיֶ֣ה] עִם־שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה וִֽיגַדֵּל֙ אֶת־כִּסְא֔וֹ מִ֨כִּסֵּ֔א אֲדֹנִ֖י הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: As the Lord hath been with my lord the king, so be he with Solomon, and make his throne higher than the throne of my lord king David.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin Herra on ollut herrani kuninkaan kanssa, niin olkoon hän myös Salomon kanssa, ja tehköön hänen istuimensa suuremmaksi herrani, kuningas Davidin, istuinta.
verses.king_james: As the Lord hath been with my lord the king, even so be he with Solomon, and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord king David.

verses.latin_vulgate: Descendit ergo Sadoc sacerdos, et Nathan propheta, et Banaias filius Jojadæ, et Cerethi, et Phelethi : et imposuerunt Salomonem super mulam regis David, et adduxerunt eum in Gihon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֣רֶד צָד֣וֹק הַ֠כֹּהֵ֠ן וְנָתָ֨ן הַנָּבִ֜יא וּבְנָיָ֣הוּ בֶן־יְהוֹיָדָ֗ע וְהַכְּרֵתִי֙ וְהַפְּלֵתִ֔י וַיַּרְכִּ֙בוּ֙ אֶת־שְׁלֹמֹ֔ה עַל־פִּרְדַּ֖ת הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ דָּוִ֑ד וַיֹּלִ֥כוּ אֹת֖וֹ עַל־גִּחֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Sadoc, the priest, and Nathan, the prophet, went down, and Banaias, the son of Joiada, and the Cerethi, and Phelethi: and they set Solomon upon the mule of king David, and brought him to Gihon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja pappi Zadok, ja Natan propheta ja BenaJa Jojadan poika ja Kreti ja Pleti menivät alas, ja asettivat Salomon kuningas Davidin muulin päälle ja veivät hänen Gihoniin.
verses.king_james: So Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, went down, and caused Solomon to ride upon king David’s mule, and brought him to Gihon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sumpsitque Sadoc sacerdos cornu olei de tabernaculo, et unxit Salomonem : et cecinerunt buccina, et dixit omnis populus : Vivat rex Salomon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּח֩ צָד֨וֹק הַכֹּהֵ֜ן אֶת־קֶ֤רֶן הַשֶּׁ֙מֶן֙ מִן־הָאֹ֔הֶל וַיִּמְשַׁ֖ח אֶת־שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה וַֽיִּתְקְעוּ֙ בַּשּׁוֹפָ֔ר וַיֹּֽאמְרוּ֙ כׇּל־הָעָ֔ם יְחִ֖י הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Sadoc, the priest, took a horn of oil out of the tabernacle, and anointed Solomon: and they sounded the trumpet, and all the people said: God save king Solomon.
verses.finnish_biblia: ja pappi Zadok otti öljysarven majasta ja voiteli Salomon, ja he soittivat basunalla, ja kaikki kansa sanoi: onneksi kuningas Salomolle!
verses.king_james: And Zadok the priest took an horn of oil out of the tabernacle, and anointed Solomon. And they blew the trumpet; and all the people said, God save king Solomon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendit universa multitudo post eum, et populus canentium tibiis, et lætantium gaudio magno : et insonuit terra a clamore eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲל֤וּ כׇל־הָעָם֙ אַחֲרָ֔יו וְהָעָם֙ מְחַלְּלִ֣ים בַּחֲלִלִ֔ים וּשְׂמֵחִ֖ים שִׂמְחָ֣ה גְדוֹלָ֑ה וַתִּבָּקַ֥ע הָאָ֖רֶץ בְּקוֹלָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all the multitude went up after him, and the people played with pipes, and rejoiced with a great joy, and the earth rang with the noise of their cry.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki kansa meni ylös hänen jälkeensä, ja kansa soitti huilulla, ja he olivat sangen suuresti iloissansa, että maa vapisi heidän äänestänsä.
verses.king_james: And all the people came up after him, and the people piped with pipes, and rejoiced with great joy, so that the earth rent with the sound of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivit autem Adonias, et omnes qui invitati fuerant ab eo : jamque convivium finitum erat : sed et Joab, audita voce tubæ, ait : Quid sibi vult clamor civitatis tumultuantis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֣ע אֲדֹנִיָּ֗הוּ וְכׇל־הַקְּרֻאִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִתּ֔וֹ וְהֵ֖ם כִּלּ֣וּ לֶאֱכֹ֑ל וַיִּשְׁמַ֤ע יוֹאָב֙ אֶת־ק֣וֹל הַשּׁוֹפָ֔ר וַיֹּ֕אמֶר מַדּ֥וּעַ קֽוֹל־הַקִּרְיָ֖ה הוֹמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Adonias, and all that were invited by him, heard it, and now the feast was at an end. Joab also, hearing the sound of the trumpet, said: What meaneth this noise of the city in an uproar?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Adonia ja kaikki kutsutut, jotka hänen kanssansa olivat, kuulivat sen, ja he olivat jo atrioineet; ja kuin Joab kuuli basunan äänen, sanoi hän: mitä se meteli ja töminä tietää kaupungissa?
verses.king_james: And Adonijah and all the guests that were with him heard it as they had made an end of eating. And when Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, he said, Wherefore is this noise of the city being in an uproar?

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc illo loquente, Jonathas filius Abiathar sacerdotis venit : cui dixit Adonias : Ingredere, quia vir fortis es, et bona nuntians.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עוֹדֶ֣נּוּ מְדַבֵּ֔ר וְהִנֵּ֧ה יוֹנָתָ֛ן בֶּן־אֶבְיָתָ֥ר הַכֹּהֵ֖ן בָּ֑א וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֲדֹנִיָּ֙הוּ֙ בֹּ֔א כִּ֣י אִ֥ישׁ חַ֛יִל אַ֖תָּה וְט֥וֹב תְּבַשֵּֽׂר׃
verses.douay_rheims: While he yet spoke, Jonathan, the son of Abiathar, the priest, came: and Adonias said to him: Come in, because thou art a valiant man, and bringest good news.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän vielä puhui, katso, niin tuli Jonatan papin AbJatarin poika. Ja Adonia sanoi: tule, sillä sinä olet luja mies ja tuot hyviä sanomia.
verses.king_james: And while he yet spake, behold, Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest came: and Adonijah said unto him, Come in; for thou art a valiant man, and bringest good tidings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responditque Jonathas Adoniæ : Nequaquam : dominus enim noster rex David regem constituit Salomonem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֙עַן֙ יֽוֹנָתָ֔ן וַיֹּ֖אמֶר לַאֲדֹנִיָּ֑הוּ אֲבָ֕ל אֲדֹנֵ֥ינוּ הַמֶּלֶךְ־דָּוִ֖ד הִמְלִ֥יךְ אֶת־שְׁלֹמֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonathan answered Adonias: Not so: for our lord, king David, hath appointed Solomon king;
verses.finnish_biblia: Jonatan vastasi ja sanoi Adonialle: totisesti, herramme kuningas David on tehnyt Salomon kuninkaaksi.
verses.king_james: And Jonathan answered and said to Adonijah, Verily our lord king David hath made Solomon king.

verses.latin_vulgate: misitque cum eo Sadoc sacerdotem, et Nathan prophetam, et Banaiam filium Jojadæ, et Cerethi, et Phelethi, et imposuerunt eum super mulam regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח אִתּֽוֹ־הַ֠מֶּ֠לֶךְ אֶת־צָד֨וֹק הַכֹּהֵ֜ן וְאֶת־נָתָ֣ן הַנָּבִ֗יא וּבְנָיָ֙הוּ֙ בֶּן־יְה֣וֹיָדָ֔ע וְהַכְּרֵתִ֖י וְהַפְּלֵתִ֑י וַיַּרְכִּ֣בוּ אֹת֔וֹ עַ֖ל פִּרְדַּ֥ת הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And hath sent with him Sadoc, the priest, and Nathan, the prophet, and Banaias, the son of Joiada, and the Cerethi, and the Phelethi, and they have set him upon the king's mule:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas on lähettänyt hänen kanssansa papin Zadokin, ja prophetan Natanin, ja BenaJan Jojadan pojan, ja Kretit ja Pletit: he ovat hänen istuttaneet kuninkaan muulin päälle.
verses.king_james: And the king hath sent with him Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, and they have caused him to ride upon the king’s mule:

verses.latin_vulgate: Unxeruntque eum Sadoc sacerdos et Nathan propheta regem in Gihon : et ascenderunt inde lætantes, et insonuit civitas : hæc est vox quam audistis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּמְשְׁח֣וּ אֹת֡וֹ צָד֣וֹק הַכֹּהֵ֣ן וְנָתָן֩ הַנָּבִ֨יא ׀ לְמֶ֜לֶךְ בְּגִח֗וֹן וַיַּעֲל֤וּ מִשָּׁם֙ שְׂמֵחִ֔ים וַתֵּהֹ֖ם הַקִּרְיָ֑ה ה֥וּא הַקּ֖וֹל אֲשֶׁ֥ר שְׁמַעְתֶּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Sadoc, the priest, and Nathan, the prophet, have anointed him king, in Gihon: and they are gone up from thence rejoicing, so that the city rang again: this is the noise that you have heard.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Zadok pappi ja Natan propheta ovat voidelleet hänen kuninkaaksi Gihonissa, ja he ovat sieltä menneet ylös iloissansa, niin että kaupunki tömisi. Se oli se meteli, kuin te kuulitte.
verses.king_james: And Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king in Gihon: and they are come up from thence rejoicing, so that the city rang again. This is the noise that ye have heard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et Salomon sedet super solium regni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם֙ יָשַׁ֣ב שְׁלֹמֹ֔ה עַ֖ל כִּסֵּ֥א הַמְּלוּכָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Moreover, Solomon sitteth upon the throne of the kingdom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vielä sitte istuu Salomo valtakunnan istuimella.
verses.king_james: And also Solomon sitteth on the throne of the kingdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingressi servi regis benedixerunt domino nostro regi David, dicentes : Amplificet Deus nomen Salomonis super nomen tuum, et magnificet thronus ejus super thronum tuum. Et adoravit rex in lectulo suo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם־בָּ֜אוּ עַבְדֵ֣י הַמֶּ֗לֶךְ לְ֠בָרֵ֠ךְ אֶת־אֲדֹנֵ֜ינוּ הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ דָּוִד֮ לֵאמֹר֒ יֵיטֵ֨ב (אלהיך) [אֱלֹהִ֜ים] אֶת־שֵׁ֤ם שְׁלֹמֹה֙ מִשְּׁמֶ֔ךָ וִיגַדֵּ֥ל אֶת־כִּסְא֖וֹ מִכִּסְאֶ֑ךָ וַיִּשְׁתַּ֥חוּ הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ עַל־הַמִּשְׁכָּֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king's servants going in, have blessed our lord king David, saying: May God make the name of Solomon greater than thy name, and make his throne greater than thy throne. And the king adored in his bed:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuninkaan palveliat ovat myös menneet siunaamaan herraamme kuningas Davidia, sanoen: antakoon sinun Jumalas Salomolle paremman nimen kuin sinun nimes on, ja tehköön hänen istuimensa suuremmaksi kuin sinun istuimes on! Ja kuningas rukoili vuoteessansa.
verses.king_james: And moreover the king’s servants came to bless our lord king David, saying, God make the name of Solomon better than thy name, and make his throne greater than thy throne. And the king bowed himself upon the bed.

verses.latin_vulgate: et locutus est : Benedictus Dominus Deus Israël, qui dedit hodie sedentem in solio meo, videntibus oculis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם־כָּ֖כָה אָמַ֣ר הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ בָּר֨וּךְ יְהֹוָ֜ה אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל אֲשֶׁ֨ר נָתַ֥ן הַיּ֛וֹם יֹשֵׁ֥ב עַל־כִּסְאִ֖י וְעֵינַ֥י רֹאֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, who hath given this day one to sit on my throne, my eyes seeing it.
verses.finnish_biblia: On myös kuningas näin sanonut: kiitetty olkoon Herra Israelin Jumala, joka tänäpänä on antanut vielä istuvan minun istuimellani, että minun silmäni sen nähneet ovat!
verses.king_james: And also thus said the king, Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, which hath given one to sit on my throne this day, mine eyes even seeing it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Territi sunt ergo, et surrexerunt omnes qui invitati fuerant ab Adonia, et ivit unusquisque in viam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֶּֽחֶרְדוּ֙ וַיָּקֻ֔מוּ כׇּ֨ל־הַקְּרֻאִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֖ר לַאֲדֹנִיָּ֑הוּ וַיֵּלְכ֖וּ אִ֥ישׁ לְדַרְכּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then all the guests of Adonias were afraid, and they all arose, and every man went his way.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hämmästyivät ja nousivat kaikki jotka Adonia kutsunut oli, ja menivät itsekukin tiellensä.
verses.king_james: And all the guests that were with Adonijah were afraid, and rose up, and went every man his way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adonias autem timens Salomonem, surrexit, et abiit, tenuitque cornu altaris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲדֹ֣נִיָּ֔הוּ יָרֵ֖א מִפְּנֵ֣י שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה וַיָּ֣קׇם וַיֵּ֔לֶךְ וַֽיַּחֲזֵ֖ק בְּקַרְנ֥וֹת הַמִּזְבֵּֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Adonias fearing Solomon, arose and went, and took hold of the horn of the altar.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan Adonia pelkäsi Salomoa, nousi ja meni ja tarttui alttarin sarviin.
verses.king_james: And Adonijah feared because of Solomon, and arose, and went, and caught hold on the horns of the altar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nuntiaverunt Salomoni, dicentes : Ecce Adonias timens regem Salomonem, tenuit cornu altaris, dicens : Juret mihi rex Salomon hodie, quod non interficiat servum suum gladio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֻּגַּ֤ד לִשְׁלֹמֹה֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הִנֵּה֙ אֲדֹ֣נִיָּ֔הוּ יָרֵ֖א אֶת־הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה וְ֠הִנֵּ֠ה אָחַ֞ז בְּקַרְנ֤וֹת הַמִּזְבֵּ֙חַ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר יִשָּׁבַֽע־לִ֤י כַיּוֹם֙ הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹ֔ה אִם־יָמִ֥ית אֶת־עַבְדּ֖וֹ בֶּחָֽרֶב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they told Solomon, saying: Behold Adonias fearing king Solomon, hath taken hold of the horn of the altar, saying: Let king Solomon swear to me this day, that he will not kill his servant with the sword.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja se ilmoitettiin Salomolle, sanoen: katso, Adonia pelkää kuningas Salomoa, ja katso, hän tarttui alttarin sarviin ja sanoi: vannokaan kuningas Salomo minulle tänäpänä, ettei hän lyö palveliaansa kuoliaaksi miekalla.
verses.king_james: And it was told Solomon, saying, Behold, Adonijah feareth king Solomon: for, lo, he hath caught hold on the horns of the altar, saying, Let king Solomon swear unto me to day that he will not slay his servant with the sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Salomon : Si fuerit vir bonus, non cadet ne unus quidem capillus ejus in terram : sin autem malum inventum fuerit in eo, morietur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שְׁלֹמֹ֔ה אִ֚ם יִהְיֶ֣ה לְבֶן־חַ֔יִל לֹא־יִפֹּ֥ל מִשַּׂעֲרָת֖וֹ אָ֑רְצָה וְאִם־רָעָ֥ה תִמָּֽצֵא־ב֖וֹ וָמֵֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Solomon said: If he be a good man, there shall not so much as one hair of his head fall to the ground: but if evil be found in him, he shall die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Salomo sanoi: jos hän tahtoo olla toimellinen mies, niin ei pidä yksikään karva hänestä lankeeman maan päälle; mutta jos jotain pahuutta hänessä löydetään, niin hänen pitää kuoleman.
verses.king_james: And Solomon said, If he will shew himself a worthy man, there shall not an hair of him fall to the earth: but if wickedness shall be found in him, he shall die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit ergo rex Salomon, et eduxit eum ab altari : et ingressus adoravit regem Salomonem : dixitque ei Salomon : Vade in domum tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֞ח הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹ֗ה וַיּוֹרִדֻ֙הוּ֙ מֵעַ֣ל הַמִּזְבֵּ֔חַ וַיָּבֹ֕א וַיִּשְׁתַּ֖חוּ לַמֶּ֣לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה וַיֹּאמֶר־ל֥וֹ שְׁלֹמֹ֖ה לֵ֥ךְ לְבֵיתֶֽךָ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Then king Solomon sent, and brought him out from the altar: and going in, he worshipped king Solomon: and Solomon said to him: Go to thy house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas Salomo lähetti ja tuotti hänen alas alttarilta, ja kuin hän tuli, rukoili hän kuningas Salomoa; mutta Salomo sanoi hänelle: mene huoneeses.
verses.king_james: So king Solomon sent, and they brought him down from the altar. And he came and bowed himself to king Solomon: and Solomon said unto him, Go to thine house.
